records.ety: [L. recantare, recantatum, to recall, recant; pref. re- re- + cantare to sing, to sound. See 3d Cant, Chant.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Recanted; p. pr. & vb. n. Recanting.]

defs: To withdraw or repudiate formally and publicly (opinions formerly expressed); to contradict, as a former declaration; to take back openly; to retract; to recall.
quotes: How soon . . . ease would recant Vows made in pain, as violent and void!

defs: To revoke a declaration or proposition; to unsay what has been said; to retract; as, convince me that I am wrong, and I will recant.
